task_completion.description: Verify the agent logged into the Splunk Web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Splunk Web interface is visible in a web browser navigated to localhost:8000.

task_completion.description: Verify a scheduled report named 'Index_Latency_Summary' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A report named 'Index_Latency_Summary' is visible in the Splunk Reports list or save dialog, with an indication that it is scheduled (e.g., a time interval or cron schedule is set).

task_completion.description: Verify the report calculates ingestion latency and aggregates by index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The search query for the report includes a calculation for latency (e.g., 'eval latency = _indextime - _time') and an aggregation command grouping by index (e.g., 'stats avg(latency) by index').

task_completion.description: Verify an alert named 'Critical_Latency_Alert' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An alert named 'Critical_Latency_Alert' is visible in the Splunk Alerts list or the alert configuration screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the alert is configured to trigger when latency exceeds 3600 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The alert's trigger condition or underlying search query explicitly checks for latency greater than 3600 (e.g., 'where latency > 3600' or a custom trigger condition set to > 3600).

task_completion.description: Verify a dashboard named 'Data_Quality_Monitoring' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dashboard titled 'Data_Quality_Monitoring' is visible in the Splunk Dashboards list or is currently open.

task_completion.description: Verify the dashboard contains a visualization panel showing latency metrics by index.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Data_Quality_Monitoring' dashboard contains at least one rendered panel (such as a chart, graph, or table) that displays latency data categorized or grouped by index.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Splunk Web UI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Splunk web interface in a browser, rather than API calls via curl or Python scripts.

integrity.description: Verify the artifacts are genuine Splunk objects.
integrity.visual_evidence: The reports, alerts, and dashboards must appear within the actual Splunk application interface, not as text files, HTML mockups, or notes in an editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent directly modifying Splunk .conf files (like savedsearches.conf) in a terminal to create the objects.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected report name: 'Index_Latency_Summary'. Expected alert name: 'Critical_Latency_Alert'. Expected dashboard name: 'Data_Quality_Monitoring'. Alert latency threshold: 3600 seconds. The latency calculation must involve the difference between _indextime and _time.
